titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick Carey to Dr Michael Dillon, 30 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick Carey

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Patrick Carey to Dr Michael Dillon regarding Dr Dillon's extended absence and requesting his resignation as a result.
Patrick Carey (1853-1934) was the Union Clerk for the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians. Poor law unions and their workhouses were established by the Poor Law (Ireland) Act, 1838, as a response to widespread poverty, and were each governed by a Board of Guardians. This letter comes from the Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians Minute Book.
Dr Michael Dillon (1862-1916) was a local medical practitioner. Residing in Rathmore, Co. Kerry, Dr Dillon passed away 3 months prior to this letter being sent.

msIdentifier.collection: Killarney Poor Law Board of Guardians BG104/B/4

correspAction.placeName: Bleak House, Rathmore, Co. Kerry, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
30th Novr 1915 Dear Sir, I am directed by the Board of Guardians to
direct your attention to the following Resolution
adopted at their meeting on the 20th Inst:- "Resolved:- That owing to his long
absence on sick leave, that Dr Dillon Medical Officer
of the Coom Dispensary District, be asked to tender
his resignation". Kindly reply for the Guardians' meeting to
be held on the 4th December, Yours faithfully,
Clerk of Union. Dr. M. Dillon,
Bleak House,
Rathmore,
